module-1-product-starter-sheet-project.label: Module 1 Product Starter Sheet Project
module-1-product-starter-sheet-project.summary: Build a simple product starter sheet that becomes clearer with each step.

mp-1-preview-product-sheet.title: Preview the core product fields
mp-1-preview-product-sheet.prompt: Begin the Module 1 Product Starter Sheet Project. Return `id, name, category` from `products` in that column order.
mp-1-preview-product-sheet.hint: Add one more field to the working SELECT list.

help.concept: Read the brief as a result contract: identify the source table or join path, the result columns, the row condition or grouping rule, and any required ordering.
help.hint_1: Build the result one clause at a time and run it after each meaningful change.
help.hint_2: Return `id, name, category` from `products` in that column order.

mp-1-preview-product-sheet.starterCode:
-- Returning id, name, and category from the products table.
SELECT
  id,
  name
FROM products;

mp-2-add-pricing-columns.title: Add the pricing field
mp-2-add-pricing-columns.prompt: Extend the Module 1 Product Starter Sheet Project with the next approved requirement. Return `id, name, category, price` from `products` in that column order.
mp-2-add-pricing-columns.hint: Extend the same query instead of rewriting it.

help.concept: Read the brief as a result contract: identify the source table or join path, the result columns, the row condition or grouping rule, and any required ordering.
help.hint_1: Build the result one clause at a time and run it after each meaningful change.
help.hint_2: Return `id, name, category, price` from `products` in that column order.

mp-2-add-pricing-columns.starterCode:
-- Keep the working query above.
-- Add price before FROM.
SELECT
  id,
  name,
  category
FROM products;

mp-3-finish-product-starter-sheet.title: Finish the starter sheet
mp-3-finish-product-starter-sheet.prompt: Complete the Module 1 Product Starter Sheet Project. Return `id, name, category, price, stock` from `products` in that column order.
mp-3-finish-product-starter-sheet.hint: Insert the final field into the same SELECT list.

help.concept: Read the brief as a result contract: identify the source table or join path, the result columns, the row condition or grouping rule, and any required ordering.
help.hint_1: Build the result one clause at a time and run it after each meaningful change.
help.hint_2: Return `id, name, category, price, stock` from `products` in that column order.

mp-3-finish-product-starter-sheet.starterCode:
-- Keep the working query above.
-- Add stock after price.
SELECT
  id,
  name,
  category,
  price
FROM products;

try_module_1_product_starter_sheet_proje.title: Preview the Product Sheet Fields
try_module_1_product_starter_sheet_proje.prompt: Before building the product sheet, create its base catalog view. Return `id`, `name`, and `category` from `products`.

module-1-product-starter-sheet-project.label: Module 1 Product Starter Sheet Project
module-1-product-starter-sheet-project.summary: Build a simple product starter sheet that becomes clearer with each step.

mp-1-preview-product-sheet.title: Preview the core product fields
mp-1-preview-product-sheet.prompt: Start the product sheet by returning `id`, `name`, and `category` from the `products` table.
mp-1-preview-product-sheet.hint: Add one more field to the working SELECT list.

help.concept: This step shapes the product sheet into a clearer report.
help.hint_1: Keep `id` and `name`, then add `category` before `FROM products`.
help.hint_2: Run the query and make sure the result shows the three requested columns.

mp-1-preview-product-sheet.starterCode:
-- Returning id, name, and category from the products table.
SELECT
  id,
  name
FROM products;

mp-2-add-pricing-columns.title: Add the pricing field
mp-2-add-pricing-columns.prompt: Add `price` so the report shows category and price together.
mp-2-add-pricing-columns.hint: Extend the same query instead of rewriting it.

help.concept: Add the new field while keeping the parts of the sheet that already read well.
help.hint_2: Add `price` before `FROM products`, then rerun the query.

mp-2-add-pricing-columns.starterCode:
-- Keep the working query above.
-- Add price before FROM.
SELECT
  id,
  name,
  category
FROM products;

mp-3-finish-product-starter-sheet.title: Finish the starter sheet
mp-3-finish-product-starter-sheet.prompt: Add `stock` so the final query returns `id`, `name`, `category`, `price`, and `stock`.
mp-3-finish-product-starter-sheet.hint: Insert the final field into the same SELECT list.

help.concept: This last step finishes the starter sheet with the final missing field.
help.hint_1: Do not remove any fields you already added.
help.hint_2: Add `stock` after `price`, then rerun the query.

mp-3-finish-product-starter-sheet.starterCode:
-- Keep the working query above.
-- Add stock after price.
SELECT
  id,
  name,
  category,
  price
FROM products;

try-module-1-product-starter-sheet-proje.title: Preview the Product Sheet Fields
try-module-1-product-starter-sheet-proje.prompt: Before building the product sheet, create its base catalog view. Return `id`, `name`, and `category` from `products`.
try-module-1-product-starter-sheet-proje.hint: Select the three requested columns in the stated order from `products`.

help.concept: This warm-up establishes the base columns used by the product-sheet project.
help.hint_1: Check the selected columns and table name in the SQL editor.
help.hint_2: Run the query and compare the result table with the expected result.

try-module-1-product-starter-sheet-proje.starterCode:
-- Return id, name, category from the products table.
SELECT
  id,
  name
FROM products;

try_module_1_product_starter_sheet_proje.title: Preview the Product Sheet Fields
try_module_1_product_starter_sheet_proje.prompt: Before building the product sheet, create its base catalog view. Return `id`, `name`, and `category` from `products`.

sketch_project_brief.title: Project brief
sketch_project_brief.bodyMarkdown:
A storefront catalog export needs a compact product sheet that is easy to scan.

Begin with the core product fields, then add the details needed for a useful catalog reference.

The finished query should produce a clean product overview ready for review.
